title: Rate problems
description: Use rates to solve word problems. For example, Charlie can type 675 words in 9 minutes. How many words can Charlie type in 13 minutes?
topic_path: Get ready for working with units

assessment_items.question_content:
At the market, $8$ batteries cost $\$10$.

**How much do $6$ batteries cost?**

$\$ $ [[☃ input-number 1]]
hints:
We’re trying to find the cost of $6$ batteries.

Batteries | Cost (dollars)
:-: | :-:
$\redE{8}$ | $\maroonE{10}$
$\downarrow \div 8$ | $\downarrow \div 8$
$1$ | $1.25$

If $\redE{8}$ batteries cost $\maroonE{\$10}$, then $1$ battery costs $\$1.25$.

Batteries | Cost (dollars)
:-: | :-:
$\redE{8}$ | $\maroonE{10}$
$1$ | $1.25$
$\downarrow \times 6$ | $\downarrow \times 6$
$\purpleD{6}$ | $\goldE{7.50}$
**So, $\purpleD{6}$ batteries cost $\goldE{\$7.50}$.**

assessment_items.question_content:
At the market, $5$ light bulbs cost $\$9$.

**How much do $7$ light bulbs cost?**

$\$ $ [[☃ input-number 1]]
hints:
We’re trying to find the cost of $7$ light bulbs.

Light bulbs | Cost (dollars)
:-: | :-:
$\redE{5}$ | $\maroonE{9.00}$
$\downarrow \div 5$ | $\downarrow \div 5$
$1$ | $1.80$

If $\redE{5}$ light bulbs cost $\maroonE{\$9}$, then $1$ light bulb costs $\$1.80$.

Light bulbs | Cost (dollars)
:-: | :-:
$\redE{5}$ | $\maroonE{9.00}$
$1$ | $1.80$
$\downarrow \times 7$ | $\downarrow \times 7$
$\purpleD{7}$ | $\goldE{12.60}$
**So, $\purpleD{7}$ light bulbs cost $\goldE{\$12.60}$.**

assessment_items.question_content:
A plane flew at a constant speed and traveled $762$ miles in $5$ hours.  

**How many miles would the plane travel in $3$ hours at the same speed?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] miles
hints:
We’re trying to find the number of miles the plane could travel in $3$ hours.

Hours | Miles
:-: | :-:
$\redE{5}$ | $\maroonE{762}$
$\downarrow \div 5$ | $\downarrow \div 5$
$1$ | $152.4$

If in $\redE{5}$ hours, the plane can travel $\maroonE{762}$ miles, then in $1$ hour, the plane can travel $152.4$ miles.

Hours | Miles
:-: | :-:
$\redE{5}$ | $\maroonE{762}$
$1$ | $152.4$
$\downarrow \times 3$ | $\downarrow \times 3$
$\purpleD{3}$ | $\goldE{457.2}$
**So, in $\purpleD{3}$ hours, the plane can travel $\goldE{457.2}$ miles.**

assessment_items.question_content:
At the market, $8$ apples cost $\$4$.

**How much do $9$ apples cost?**

$\$ $ [[☃ input-number 1]]
hints:
We’re trying to find the cost of $9$ apples.

Apples | Cost (dollars)
:-: | :-:
$\redE{8}$ | $\maroonE{4.00}$
$\downarrow \div 8$ | $\downarrow \div 8$
$1$ | $0.50$

If $\redE{8}$ apples cost $\maroonE{\$4}$, then $1$ apple costs $\$0.50$.

Apples | Cost (dollars)
:-: | :-:
$\redE{8}$ | $\maroonE{4.00}$
$1$ | $0.50$
$\downarrow \times 9$ | $\downarrow \times 9$
$\purpleD{9}$ | $\goldE{4.50}$
**So, $\purpleD{9}$ apples cost $\goldE{\$4.50}$.**

assessment_items.question_content:
Amanda can jog $18$ miles in $5$ hours.  

**At this rate,  how many miles can Amanda jog in $4$ hours?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] miles
hints:
We’re trying to find the number of miles Amanda can jog in $4$ hours.

Hours | Miles
:-: | :-:
$\redE{5}$ | $\maroonE{18}$
$\downarrow \div 5$ | $\downarrow \div 5$
$1$ | $3\dfrac{3}{5}$

If in $\redE{5}$ hours Amanda can jog $\maroonE{18}$ miles, then in $1$ hour she can jog $3\dfrac35$ miles.

Hours | Miles
:-: | :-:
$\redE{5}$ | $\maroonE{18}$
$1$ | $3\dfrac{3}{5}$
$\downarrow \times 4$ | $\downarrow \times 4$
$\purpleD{4}$ | $\goldE{14\dfrac{2}{5}}$
**So, in $\purpleD{4}$ hours, Amanda can jog $\goldE{14\dfrac25}$ miles.**

assessment_items.question_content:
Henry can write $5$ pages of his novel in $3$ hours.  

**At this rate, how many pages can Henry write in $8$ hours?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] pages
hints:
We’re trying to find the number of pages Henry can write in $8$ hours.

Hours | Pages
:-: | :-:
$\redE{3}$ | $\maroonE{5}$
$\downarrow \div 3$ | $\downarrow \div 3$
$1$ | $1\dfrac{2}{3}$

If in $\redE{3}$ hours Henry can write $\maroonE{5}$ pages, then in $1$ hour he can write $1\dfrac23$ pages.

Hours | Pages
:-: | :-:
$\redE{3}$ | $\maroonE{5}$
$1$ | $1\dfrac{2}{3}$
$\downarrow \times 8$ | $\downarrow \times 8$
$\purpleD{8}$ | $\goldE{13 \dfrac{1}{3}}$

A fractional number of pages makes sense, because it is possible to write part of a page.
**So, in $\purpleD{8}$ hours, Henry can write $\goldE{13\dfrac13}$ or $\goldE{\dfrac{40}{3}}$ pages.**

assessment_items.question_content:
Tommy can exchange $8$ euros for $11$ dollars.  

**At this rate, how many dollars can Tommy get with $12$ euros?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] dollars
hints:
We’re trying to find the number of dollars Tommy can get with $12$ euros.

Euros | Dollars
:-: | :-:
$\redE{8}$ | $\maroonE{11}$
$\downarrow \div 8$ | $\downarrow \div 8$
$1$ | $1\dfrac{3}{8}$

If Tommy can exchange $\redE{8}$ euros for $\maroonE{11}$ dollars, then he can exchange $1$ euro for $1\dfrac38$ dollars.

Euros | Dollars
:-: | :-:
$\redE{8}$ | $\maroonE{11}$
$1$ | $1\dfrac{3}{8}$
$\downarrow \times 12$ | $\downarrow \times 12$
$\purpleD{12}$ | $\goldE{16\dfrac{1}{2}}$
**So, with $\purpleD{12}$ euros, Tommy can exchange for $\goldE{16\dfrac12}$ or $\goldE{16.50}$ dollars.**

assessment_items.question_content:
Heather is a cashier. She can ring up  $12$ customers in $9$ minutes.  

**At this rate, how many minutes does it take her to ring up $4$ customers?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] minutes
hints:
We’re trying to find the number of minutes it takes Heather to ring up $4$ customers.  

Customers | Minutes
:-: | :-:
$\redE{12}$ | $\maroonE{9}$
$\downarrow \div 12$ | $\downarrow \div 12$
$1$ | $\dfrac{3}{4}$

If Heather can ring up $\redE{12}$ customers in $\maroonE{9}$ minutes, then she can ring up $1$ customer in $\dfrac34$ of a minute.

Customers | Minutes
:-: | :-:
$\redE{12}$ | $\maroonE{9}$
$1$ | $\dfrac{3}{4}$
$\downarrow \times 4$ | $\downarrow \times 4$
$\purpleD{4}$ | $\goldE{3}$
**So, Heather can ring up $\purpleD{4}$ customers in $\goldE{3}$ minutes.**

assessment_items.question_content:
Luke can paint $91$ portraits in $7$ weeks.

**How many portraits can Luke paint in $4$ weeks?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] portraits
hints:
We’re trying to find the number of portraits Luke can paint in $4$ weeks.

Weeks | Portraits
:-: | :-:
$\redE{7}$ | $\maroonE{91}$
$\downarrow \div 7$ | $\downarrow \div 7$
$1$ | $13$

If in $\redE{7}$ weeks, Luke can paint $\maroonE{91}$ portraits, then in $1$ week, he can paint $13$ portraits.

Weeks | Portraits
:-: | :-:
$\redE{7}$ | $\maroonE{91}$
$1$ | $13$
$\downarrow \times 4$ | $\downarrow \times 4$
$\purpleD{4}$ | $\goldE{52}$
**So, in $\purpleD{4}$ weeks, Luke can paint $\goldE{52}$ portraits.**

assessment_items.question_content:
Charles can type $675$ words in $9$ minutes.  

**How many words can Charles type in $13$ minutes?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] words
hints:
We’re trying to find the number of words Charles can type in $13$ minutes.

Minutes | Words
:-: | :-:
$\redE{9}$ | $\maroonE{675}$
$\downarrow \div 9$ | $\downarrow \div 9$
$1$ | $75$

If in $\redE{9}$ minutes, Charles can type $\maroonE{675}$ words, then in $1$ minute, he can type $75$ words.

Minutes | Words
:-: | :-:
$\redE{9}$ | $\maroonE{675}$
$1$ | $75$
$\downarrow \times 13$ | $\downarrow \times 13$
$\purpleD{13}$ | $\goldE{975}$
**So, in $\purpleD{13}$ minutes, Charles can type $\goldE{975}$ words.**

assessment_items.question_content:
Bill can hit a bucket of $323$ golf balls in $17$ hours.

**How many golf balls can Bill hit in $23$ hours?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] golf balls
hints:
We’re trying to find the number of golf balls Bill can hit in $23$ hours.

Hours | Golf balls
:-: | :-:
$\redE{17}$ | $\maroonE{323}$
$\downarrow \div 17$ | $\downarrow \div 17$
$1$ | $19$

If in $\redE{17}$ hours, Bill can hit $\maroonE{323}$ golf balls, then in $1$ hour, he can hit $19$ golf balls.

Hours | Golf balls
:-: | :-:
$\redE{17}$ | $\maroonE{323}$
$1$ | $19$
$\downarrow \times 23$ | $\downarrow \times 23$
$\purpleD{23}$ | $\goldE{437}$
**So, in $\purpleD{23}$ hours, Bill can hit $\goldE{437}$ golf balls.**

assessment_items.question_content:
Frances can complete $91$ oil changes in $7$ days.

**How many oil changes can Frances complete in $11$ days?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] oil changes
hints:
We’re trying to find the number of oil changes Frances can complete in $11$ days.

Days | Oil changes
:-: | :-:
$\redE{7}$ | $\maroonE{91}$
$\downarrow \div 7$ | $\downarrow \div 7$
$1$ | $13$

If in $\redE{7}$ days, Frances can complete $\maroonE{91}$ oil changes, then in $1$ day, she can complete $13$ oil changes.

Days | Oil changes
:-: | :-:
$\redE{7}$ | $\maroonE{91}$
$1$ | $13$
$\downarrow \times 11$ | $\downarrow \times 11$
$\purpleD{11}$ | $\goldE{143}$
**So, in $\purpleD{11}$ days, Frances can complete $\goldE{143}$ oil changes.**

assessment_items.question_content:
Robin can clean $72$ rooms in $6$ days.

**How many rooms can Robin clean in $9$ days?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] rooms
hints:
We’re trying to find the number of rooms Robin can clean in $9$ days.

Days | Rooms
:-: | :-:
$\redE{6}$ | $\maroonE{72}$
$\downarrow \div 6$ | $\downarrow \div 6$
$1$ | $12$

If in $\redE{6}$ days, Robin can clean $\maroonE{72}$ rooms, then in $1$ day, she can clean $12$ rooms.

Days | Rooms
:-: | :-:
$\redE{6}$ | $\maroonE{72}$
$1$ | $12$
$\downarrow \times 9$ | $\downarrow \times 9$
$\purpleD{9}$ | $\goldE{108}$
**So, in $\purpleD{9}$ days, Robin can clean $\goldE{108}$ rooms.**

assessment_items.question_content:
Brenda can deliver $644$ newspapers in $7$ hours.

**How many newspapers can Brenda deliver in $9$ hours?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] newspapers
hints:
We’re trying to find the number of newspapers Brenda can deliver in $9$ hours.

Hours | Newspapers
:-: | :-:
$\redE{7}$ | $\maroonE{644}$
$\downarrow \div 7$ | $\downarrow \div 7$
$1$ | $92$

If in $\redE{7}$ hours, Brenda can deliver $\maroonE{644}$ newspapers, then in $1$ hour, she can deliver $92$ newspapers.

Hours | Newspapers
:-: | :-:
$\redE{7}$ | $\maroonE{644}$
$1$ | $92$
$\downarrow \times 9$ | $\downarrow \times 9$
$\purpleD{9}$ | $\goldE{828}$
**So, in $\purpleD{9}$ hours, Brenda can deliver $\goldE{828}$ newspapers.**

assessment_items.question_content:
Lynnette can wash $95$ cars in $5$ days.

**How many cars can Lynnette wash in $11$ days?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] cars
hints:
We’re trying to find the number of cars Lynnette can wash in $11$ days.

Days | Cars
:-: | :-:
$\redE{5}$ | $\maroonE{95}$
$\downarrow \div 5$ | $\downarrow \div 5$
$1$ | $19$

If in $\redE{5}$ days, Lynnette can wash $\maroonE{95}$ cars, then in $1$ day, she can wash $19$ cars.

Days | Cars
:-: | :-:
$\redE{5}$ | $\maroonE{95}$
$1$ | $19$
$\downarrow \times 11$ | $\downarrow \times 11$
$\purpleD{11}$ | $\goldE{209}$
**So, in $\purpleD{11}$ days, Lynnette can wash $\goldE{209}$ cars.**

assessment_items.question_content:
Alicia drove at a constant speed and traveled $183$ miles in $3$ hours.  

**How many miles would Alicia travel in $11$ hours at the same speed?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] miles
hints:
We’re trying to find the number of miles Alicia would travel in $11$ hours.

Hours | Miles
:-: | :-:
$\redE{3}$ | $\maroonE{183}$
$\downarrow \div 3$ | $\downarrow \div 3$
$1$ | $61$

If in $\redE{3}$ hours, Alicia traveled $\maroonE{183}$ miles, then in $1$ hour, she would travel $61$ miles.

Hours | Miles
:-: | :-:
$\redE{3}$ | $\maroonE{183}$
$1$ | $61$
$\downarrow \times 11$ | $\downarrow \times 11$
$\purpleD{11}$ | $\goldE{671}$
**So, in $\purpleD{11}$ hours, Alicia would travel $\goldE{671}$ miles.**

assessment_items.question_content:
Ken can walk $40$ dogs in $8$ hours.

**How many dogs can Ken walk in $12$ hours?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] dogs
hints:
We’re trying to find the number of dogs Ken can walk in $12$ hours.

Hours | Dogs
:-: | :-:
$\redE{8}$ | $\maroonE{40}$
$\downarrow \div 8$ | $\downarrow \div 8$
$1$ | $5$

If in $\redE{8}$ hours, Ken can walk $\maroonE{40}$ dogs, then in $1$ hour, he can walk $5$ dogs.

Hours | Dogs
:-: | :-:
$\redE{8}$ | $\maroonE{40}$
$1$ | $5$
$\downarrow \times 12$ | $\downarrow \times 12$
$\purpleD{12}$ | $\goldE{60}$
**So, in $\purpleD{12}$ hours, Ken can walk $\goldE{60}$ dogs.**

assessment_items.question_content:
Mark can make $42$ birthday cakes in $7$ days.

**How many birthday cakes can Mark make in $5$ days?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] birthday cakes
hints:
We’re trying to find the number of cakes Mark can make in $5$ days.

Days | Cakes
:-: | :-:
$\redE{7}$ | $\maroonE{42}$
$\downarrow \div 7$ | $\downarrow \div 7$
$1$ | $6$

If in $\redE{7}$ days, Mark can make $\maroonE{42}$ cakes, then in $1$ day, he can make $6$ cakes.

Days | Cakes
:-: | :-:
$\redE{7}$ | $\maroonE{42}$
$1$ | $6$
$\downarrow \times 5$ | $\downarrow \times 5$
$\purpleD{5}$ | $\goldE{30}$
**So, in $\purpleD{5}$ days, Mark can make $\goldE{30}$ cakes.**
